qa_pairs.question: What is the main objective of the instructions provided on this page regarding Windows virtual machines?
qa_pairs.answer: The main objective is to describe the steps required to partition and format a volume that has been attached to a Windows VM.

qa_pairs.question: After a new volume has been attached to a Windows VM, what are the initial steps to begin the partitioning and formatting process?
qa_pairs.answer: First, connect to the Windows VM using a Remote Desktop connection. Then, open "Computer Management" and navigate to "Storage" -> "Disk Management".

qa_pairs.question: In Disk Management on a Windows VM, what are the steps to prepare a newly attached disk for creating a new simple volume?
qa_pairs.answer: To prepare the disk, you must first right-click on the new disk label (likely "Disk 1") and select "online" to bring the disk online. Following that, right-click on the disk label again and select "Initialize Disk". Finally, right-click on the "unallocated" disk pane and select "create new simple volume".
